record_identity.title: Corpus coverage and repair reconciliation audit

summary: Completes a failure-record-by-failure-record comparison against current Caelestis main, adds structured corpus_coverage provenance to every failure, creates three cluster-level retrospective coverage patches, confirms formerly staged doctrine, reconciles stale repair and proposal states, and preserves four genuine open primitive gaps.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Regulators, developers, maintainers, researchers, and AI systems need to distinguish implemented repair, retrospective coverage, partial coverage, uncovered gaps, ecosystem persistence, and runtime verification without searching the corpus manually.

source_records.deployment_context: Canonical Caelestis main-branch corpus reviewed for VIGIL repair reconciliation.
source_records.source_context: Corpus snapshot used for the VIGIL coverage and repair reconciliation pass.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary current-corpus evidence for the VIGIL coverage assessment.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL draft PR #29 — evidence, lifecycle, and corpus repair reconciliation
source_records.model_or_algorithm: VIGIL schemas, records, validators, and indexes
source_records.source_context: Branch and pull request carrying the completed corpus-coverage reconciliation.
source_records.relevance_note: Implementation and validation surface for this registry repair.

system_context.interface_surface: all VIGIL failure, proposal, patch, schema, validator, and index surfaces
system_context.user_role: maintainer / regulator / developer / researcher / AI system

change_classification.change_scope: all VIGIL failure records and failure-linked repair/proposal records

changed_components: corpus_coverage added to every failure record
PATCH-0001, PATCH-0003, PATCH-0004, PATCH-0012, and PATCH-0014 reconciled
FM-0003, FM-0004, FM-0005, FM-0006, FM-0007, FM-0017, and FM-0025–FM-0027 lifecycle repaired
PROP-0006, PROP-0007, PROP-0009, and PROP-0011 resolved by patch
FM-0009, FM-0018, FM-0019, and FM-0021 preserved as genuine open gaps
schemas, templates, validators, guidance, and generated summaries updated
change_details.change_summary: Every failure now exposes a machine-searchable current-corpus assessment and the exact instruments or gaps supporting its repair status.
change_details.doctrine_change: No Caelestis doctrine was changed by this VIGIL audit.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Current-corpus review, deterministic migration, reciprocal-link validation, ordinary record validation, lifecycle and corpus-coverage validation, routing, generated-index construction, and index enrichment.
implementation_verification.verified_by: VIGIL repository automation and maintainer review
evidence: new retrospective patches contain exact coverage origins
GitHub Actions workflow run 29306577564 passed every validation and build step
implementation_verification.verification_result: passed — implemented records, reciprocal links, schemas, validators, routing, and enriched indexes validate together

impact_summary.intended_effect: Make VIGIL searchable as an evidence-to-repair observatory with honest current-corpus coverage states.
affected_records_or_components: failure, proposal, patch, schema, validator, guidance, and generated-index surfaces
impact_summary.known_limitations: This audit assesses the current CAM corpus. It does not prove external vendor adoption, runtime conformance, ecosystem resolution, or legal compliance.

remaining_work: Implement the four preserved open primitives where approved.
Add public interface filters for corpus coverage classification and covered instruments.
Continue runtime and ecosystem monitoring independently of CAM repair status.

cam_internal.governance_layer: registry observability / repair provenance / corpus coverage
cam_internal.routing_note: VIGIL-only reconciliation; no Caelestis doctrine amendment.

repair_provenance.repair_basis: Existing current-corpus coverage was systematically assessed and linked; unresolved gaps were preserved.
